passages.title: Passage 1: Cultural Heritage
passages.text: Vietnam has a rich cultural heritage, and preserving it is crucial for future generations. To conserve this heritage, the government and people must collaborate. (1) ______ of Vietnam's traditional customs, monuments, and folk art forms are (2) ______ risk due to modernization and urbanization. Through education and awareness programs, citizens can learn to appreciate and protect (3) _______. Monuments and artifacts, inspired by ancient designs, must be maintained and restored by experts. (4) _______ the rules of preservation, we can ensure that Vietnam's cultural treasures will survive for years to come. Preservation efforts involve both local and international collaboration, encouraging the exchange of knowledge and techniques. It is important (5) ________ the significance of these (6) ________ so they are not forgotten as the world moves forward.

passages.title: Passage 2: Education Paths
passages.text:
Graduation is just around the corner! Are you torn between hitting the books at a university or gaining hands-on experience through (7) ________ education?

Explore Your Options:
· Academic Track: Pursue a degree to get high-level academic qualifications.
· Vocational Track: Focus (8) ________ practical skills. Consider an (9) ________ to earn while you learn.

Expert Insights:
Choosing a career path may seem (10) ________ at first, but we are here to help.

Important Reflections:
It is the right choice of institution (11) ________ will pave the way for your future career.

We understand the pressure of final exams. (12) ________ excellent results in your studies, you now deserve the best guidance to pick the perfect path.

CONTACT US TODAY!
· Website: www.futurepathway.edu
· Hotline: 1800-CAREER
"Your qualification is your passport to the world!"

passages.title: Passage 3: Conversations
passages.text: Arrange the following conversations in correct order.

letters.a: Nam: I've been working on becoming more independent, too. I moved out of my parents' house and started managing my own finances.
letters.b: Nam: Hi, Mark! It's been a while since we last met. You seem so independent now!
letters.c: Mark: Hi, Nam! Thanks! Yes, I've been trying to take control of my life more. How about you?

letters.a: Mai: I will participate in traditional cultural activities.
letters.c: Mai: Because they help preserve the long-standing cultural values of our nation, like music, dance, and festivals.
letters.d: Lan: What are you going to do to protect Vietnamese culture?
letters.e: Lan: Yes, I think so too. If we don't protect them now, many beautiful cultural features will be lost.

letters.a: I just came back from a cultural heritage festival, and it was amazing to see so many traditional arts and crafts.
letters.b: How's everything with you? I've got some exciting news to share about my weekend!
letters.c: The performances were incredible, and I learned a lot about our history and traditions.
letters.d: I was so inspired by the experience, and I even got to meet some local artists.
letters.e: Can't wait to share all the details with you when we meet up next!

letters.a: I also looked into the online courses you mentioned — they seem very flexible and affordable, so I'm thinking of giving them a try if I decide not to attend university.
letters.b: I'm really considering applying for a vocational training course, as it sounds like a great way to gain practical skills quickly.
letters.c: However, I'm still weighing whether I should go for a university degree, as I feel it might open more career doors in the long term.
letters.d: What do you think about taking a gap year to travel before making any decisions?
letters.e: Thanks for the information about different education options for school leavers.

letters.a: In conclusion, I'm very happy with this career choice, as it perfectly aligns with my interests and skills.
letters.b: Working remotely allows me to be more creative and productive in my job.
letters.c: Although there are some challenges in coordinating with the team, online meetings make it easy for us to stay updated on project progress.
letters.d: After finishing my university program, I realized I had a passion for both technology and business, so I decided to pursue a career in digital marketing.
letters.e: I've been working at a startup company in this field for almost a year, and I truly feel satisfied with my work.

passages.title: Passage 4: Heritage Reading
passages.text: Cultural heritage plays a crucial role in preserving a nation's identity and values. It includes historical (18) ______ and traditional customs passed down through generations. It is cultural heritage that (19) ______ people understand their roots and develop national pride. Many countries (20) ______ great efforts to protect heritage because it reflects their unique history. (21) ______ many ancient sites, governments can promote cultural tourism and create economic benefits for local communities. Therefore, raising public awareness and encouraging sustainable tourism are essential to ensure the long-term (22) ______ of cultural heritage for future generations.

passages.title: Passage 5: Independence
passages.text:
Being independent is an important skill for young students. It helps them manage their time, solve problems, and take responsibility for their own learning. One way to develop independence is to practice good time management. By creating a study schedule and setting clear goals, students can stay on track and avoid procrastination. For example, breaking down big tasks into smaller ones can make them feel less overwhelming.

Another key to independence is learning to make decisions. Students should start by making small choices, such as deciding what to eat or how to organize their study materials. Gradually, they can take on bigger decisions, like choosing extracurricular activities or planning their study routine. These decisions build confidence and self-reliance.

Additionally, students can become more independent by seeking help when needed. Asking teachers, classmates, or family members for advice shows that independence does not mean doing everything alone. It's important to know when to ask for help and how to use the resources available to you.

Finally, developing a growth mindset is essential for independence. A growth mindset means believing that effort leads to improvement. By embracing challenges and learning from mistakes, students can become more resilient and capable of solving problems on their own.

(Adapted from a general approach to student independence)

options: A. Students should focus only on academic success at school
B. Independence helps students become more confident in social situations
C. Developing independence is essential for students and can be achieved through several practical ways
D. Time management is the most important skill for young students
A. Avoiding challenges to prevent mistakes
B. Making decisions without asking anyone
C. Breaking large tasks into smaller ones
A. Students should rely solely on others for help
B. A growth mindset helps students handle challenges
C. Independence means never asking for help
D. Time management is not important for independence
A. Independent students never need help from others
B. Independence requires students to handle all problems alone
C. Independent students know when to seek help and use available resources effectively
D. Independence develops naturally without practice
